findings.title: Excessive complexity in clean() (CC=17)
findings.description: `clean()` at L1099 has cyclomatic complexity 17. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in set_choices() (CC=23)
findings.description: `set_choices()` at L1202 has cyclomatic complexity 23. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via innerHTML assignment at line 6
findings.description: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML` at L6: `c=d.insertBefore(c.lastChild,d.firstChild);b.hasCSS=!!c}g||t(a,b);return a}var k=l.html5||`. An attacker can inject a `<script>` tag or event handler that executes in the victim's browser. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low)
findings.suggestion: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.

rule.title: XSS via innerHTML assignment
rule.summary: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML`.
rule.remediation: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.
known_limitations: DOM and template heuristics are strongest when flows remain inside a file or helper chain.

findings.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
findings.description: A string literal with Shannon entropy 4.58 bits was detected. High-entropy strings embedded in source code are often credentials, API keys, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., os.environ['API_KEY'], HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

rule.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
rule.summary: Flags secrets, credentials, and signing keys embedded directly in source code.
rule.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate compromised values.

findings.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
findings.description: A string literal with Shannon entropy 4.59 bits was detected. High-entropy strings embedded in source code are often credentials, API keys, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., os.environ['API_KEY'], HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

rule.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
rule.summary: Flags secrets, credentials, and signing keys embedded directly in source code.
rule.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate compromised values.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in clone() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `clone()` passes `VSI_MEM_FILESYSTEM_BASE_PATH` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L470 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in __init__() (CC=26)
findings.description: `__init__()` at L77 has cyclomatic complexity 26. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: Implicit None return in create_generic_related_manager() — can fall off end
findings.description: `create_generic_related_manager()` has 17 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in create_generic_related_manager() (CC=24)

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code Injection via eval() in shell_command()
findings.description: Untrusted data flows from `startup` (environment variable, L1020) to `eval()` at L1023 without sanitization. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary Python code in the application context.
findings.suggestion: Avoid eval/exec. Use ast.literal_eval() for data, or a safe expression evaluator.

rule.title: Python code injection via tainted dynamic execution
rule.summary: Tracks user input into `eval`, `exec`, or similar dynamic code execution sinks.
rule.remediation: Replace eval-like execution with safe parsing or explicit dispatch.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code Injection via compile() in shell_command()
findings.description: Untrusted data flows from `startup` (environment variable, L1020) to `compile()` at L1023 without sanitization. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary Python code in the application context.
findings.suggestion: Avoid eval/exec. Use ast.literal_eval() for data, or a safe expression evaluator.

rule.title: Python code injection via tainted dynamic execution
rule.summary: Tracks user input into `eval`, `exec`, or similar dynamic code execution sinks.
rule.remediation: Replace eval-like execution with safe parsing or explicit dispatch.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path Traversal via open() in shell_command()
findings.description: Untrusted data flows from `startup` (environment variable, L1020) to `open()` at L1022 without sanitization. An attacker can exploit this to read/write arbitrary files on the server.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize paths: `secure_filename(f)` then `assert resolved.startswith(BASE_DIR)`
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: with open(startup) as f:
AFTER:          safe_path = Path(BASE_DIR, startup).resolve()
                assert str(safe_path).startswith(str(BASE_DIR))
                with open(safe_path) as f:

rule.title: Python SQL injection via tainted query text
rule.summary: Tracks user input into SQL text formatting and execution calls.
rule.remediation: Switch to parameterized SQL or ORM bind parameters.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in _path_is_ancestor() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `_path_is_ancestor()` passes `path` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L695 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in shell_command() via open()
findings.description: `shell_command()` passes `startup` (user-controlled) to file open at L1022 without path sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory and read/write arbitrary files.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: with open(startup) as f:
AFTER:          safe_path = Path(BASE_DIR, startup).resolve()
                assert str(safe_path).startswith(str(BASE_DIR))
                with open(safe_path) as f:

rule.title: Python file open with tainted path
rule.summary: Flags `open()` or `Path.*` file operations that use user-controlled paths without confinement.
rule.remediation: Secure filenames and verify the resolved path remains under the expected base directory.

findings.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise in run_command()
findings.description: `run_command()` catches all exceptions at L956 without re-raising. Errors may be silently hidden in production.
findings.suggestion: Catch specific exception types, or log and re-raise broad catches.

rule.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise
rule.summary: Flags broad exception handlers that suppress unexpected failures instead of surfacing them.
rule.remediation: Catch narrower exception types or log and re-raise unexpected errors.

findings.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise in list_commands()
findings.description: `list_commands()` catches all exceptions at L650 without re-raising. Errors may be silently hidden in production.
findings.suggestion: Catch specific exception types, or log and re-raise broad catches.

rule.title: Broad exception catch without re-raise
rule.summary: Flags broad exception handlers that suppress unexpected failures instead of surfacing them.
rule.remediation: Catch narrower exception types or log and re-raise unexpected errors.

findings.description: A string literal with Shannon entropy 4.71 bits was detected. High-entropy strings embedded in source code are often credentials, API keys, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., os.environ['API_KEY'], HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

rule.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
rule.summary: Flags secrets, credentials, and signing keys embedded directly in source code.
rule.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate compromised values.

findings.title: Implicit None return in authenticate() — can fall off end
findings.description: `authenticate()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.
